beats.narration: Every discontinuity was born at one of three stages of the metal's life. That stage predicts shape, location, and orientation on film.

payload.title: BORN, MADE, EARNED
payload.sub: Every discontinuity dates from one stage of the metal's life.

beats.narration: Three classes: inherent, born in the melt; processing, made in the mill and shop; service, earned after the part goes to work.

tiles.s: Made in the mill and shop

beats.narration: Walk the timeline: solidification traps porosity, inclusions, shrinkage. Rolling, forging, welding add laps, bursts, weld cracks. In service, fatigue and corrosion move in.

payload.heading: Where discontinuities are born
payload.note: EVERY STAGE WRITES ITS OWN FILM SIGNATURE

beats.narration: Radiography sees only thickness change along the beam. Volumetric flaws print from any angle. Planar flaws print only edge-on — laminations belong to ultrasonics.

payload.heading: Orientation decides what RT can see
payload.note: LAMINATIONS ARE A JOB FOR ULTRASONICS

beats.narration: Read the traveler before you read the film: how the part was made turns a smudge of density into a short list of suspects.

payload.sub: A smudge of density becomes a short list of suspects.
